title: SpacetimeDB-Native Autonomous Inference Dispatch (ADR-2604011200)
description: Close the final AAIDE autonomy gap: replace hexflo_memory inference queue with a dedicated SpacetimeDB inference_task table. Add /ws/inference WebSocket relay in hex-nexus. Add hex inference watch sidecar. Workplan executor subscribes to STDB events instead of polling. Result: workplan phases advance without UserPromptSubmit dependency.

phases.name: STDB: inference_task table + reducers
phases.description: Add inference_task table and CRUD reducers to hexflo-coordination WASM module. This is the event-sourced backbone — all other phases depend on it.

tasks.name: Add inference_task table to hexflo-coordination
tasks.description:
In spacetime-modules/hexflo-coordination/src/lib.rs, add the inference_task table after the existing swarm_task table:

```rust
#[table(name = inference_task, public)]
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct InferenceTask {
    #[primary_key]
    pub id: String,
    pub workplan_id: String,
    pub task_id: String,
    pub phase: String,
    pub prompt: String,
    pub role: String,
    /// "Pending" | "InProgress" | "Completed" | "Failed"
    pub status: String,
    /// Agent that claimed this task (empty until claimed)
    pub agent_id: String,
    pub result: String,
    pub error: String,
    pub created_at: String,
    pub updated_at: String,
}
```

Then add these reducers:

1. `inference_task_create(ctx, id, workplan_id, task_id, phase, prompt, role, created_at)` — inserts row with status=Pending
2. `inference_task_claim(ctx, id, agent_id, updated_at)` — CAS: only transitions Pending→InProgress; return Err if already claimed
3. `inference_task_complete(ctx, id, result, updated_at)` — transitions InProgress→Completed, sets result
4. `inference_task_fail(ctx, id, error, updated_at)` — transitions any non-Completed→Failed, sets error

For `inference_task_claim`, use the same CAS pattern as `task_assign`: check status==Pending before updating, return `already_claimed:{agent_id}` error if not.
tasks.done_condition: cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p hexflo-coordination passes; inference_task table and 4 reducers present

phases.name: State port + adapters: inference_task methods
phases.description: Extend IStatePort with inference task methods. Implement in SpacetimeDB adapter (real calls) and stub adapter (Err stubs). Add InferenceTaskInfo struct to ports/state.rs.

tasks.name: Add InferenceTaskInfo and port methods to ports/state.rs
tasks.description:
In hex-nexus/src/ports/state.rs:

1. Add InferenceTaskInfo struct (derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct InferenceTaskInfo {
    pub id: String,
    pub workplan_id: String,
    pub task_id: String,
    pub phase: String,
    pub prompt: String,
    pub role: String,
    pub status: String,
    pub agent_id: String,
    pub result: String,
    pub error: String,
    pub created_at: String,
    pub updated_at: String,
}
```

2. Add to IStatePort trait (async_trait):
```rust
async fn inference_task_create(&self, id: &str, workplan_id: &str, task_id: &str, phase: &str, prompt: &str, role: &str, created_at: &str) -> Result<(), StateError>;
async fn inference_task_claim(&self, id: &str, agent_id: &str, updated_at: &str) -> Result<(), StateError>;
async fn inference_task_complete(&self, id: &str, result: &str, updated_at: &str) -> Result<(), StateError>;
async fn inference_task_fail(&self, id: &str, error: &str, updated_at: &str) -> Result<(), StateError>;
async fn inference_task_get(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<InferenceTaskInfo>, StateError>;
async fn inference_task_list_pending(&self) -> Result<Vec<InferenceTaskInfo>, StateError>;
```
tasks.done_condition: IStatePort trait compiles with new methods; InferenceTaskInfo struct present

tasks.name: Implement inference_task methods in SpacetimeDB adapter
tasks.done_condition: cargo check -p hex-nexus passes; all 6 methods implemented in both real and stub adapters

phases.name: hex-nexus: /ws/inference WebSocket relay
phases.description: Add /ws/inference WebSocket endpoint to hex-nexus. Maintain a registry of connected CC agents. When STDB fires inference_task insertion, broadcast InferenceTaskPush to all subscribers. This is the push channel that replaces UserPromptSubmit inbox polling.

tasks.name: Add InferenceWsRegistry to nexus AppState
tasks.done_condition: InferenceTxBus and InferenceTaskPush types present in AppState; cargo check passes

tasks.name: Add /ws/inference WebSocket handler and route
Wire STDB inference_task subscription to broadcast bus
tasks.description:
In hex-nexus, the STDB subscription handler already processes row updates for swarm tables. Extend it to watch inference_task insertions.

Find the STDB subscription callback (likely in adapters/spacetime_state.rs or a spawn task in lib.rs that calls subscribe_to_tables). Add inference_task to the subscribed tables:

```rust
// In subscribe query:
"SELECT * FROM inference_task"
```

In the row-update handler, when a new InferenceTask row arrives with status=="Pending":
```rust
if row.status == "Pending" {
    let push = InferenceTaskPush {
        id: row.id.clone(),
        workplan_id: row.workplan_id.clone(),
        task_id: row.task_id.clone(),
        phase: row.phase.clone(),
        prompt: row.prompt.clone(),
        role: row.role.clone(),
    };
    let _ = inference_tx.send(push); // broadcast to all /ws/inference subscribers
}
```

Also send an inbox notification (as before) for backwards compatibility with the existing hook-based path.
tasks.done_condition: STDB subscription includes inference_task; new Pending rows are broadcast on inference_tx; cargo check passes

tasks.done_condition: Path B writes to STDB via inference_task_create; hexflo_memory queue write removed; cargo check passes

tasks.name: Replace memory polling with STDB task subscription in executor
tasks.done_condition: Executor polls STDB inference_task_get instead of hexflo_memory; timeout calls inference_task_fail; cargo check passes

phases.name: hex-cli: hex inference watch command
phases.description: Add 'hex inference watch' CLI command. Connects to /ws/inference, receives InferenceTaskPush messages, claims tasks via inference_task_claim, dispatches Claude subagents (via MCP stdio or direct spawn), marks complete. This is the autonomous sidecar that closes the UserPromptSubmit gap.

tasks.name: Add hex inference watch command to CLI
tasks.description:
Create hex-cli/src/commands/inference.rs with the `watch` subcommand.

The command:
1. Reads agent_id from session file (NexusClient::from_env() auto-resolves)
2. Connects to ws://localhost:5555/ws/inference via tokio-tungstenite
3. On each InferenceTaskPush message:
   a. PATCH /api/inference/tasks/{id}/claim with agent_id (calls inference_task_claim)
   b. If claim succeeds (200): spawn `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions` with the prompt
   c. On subprocess exit: read stdout, PATCH /api/inference/tasks/{id}/complete with result
   d. If claim fails (409 Conflict): skip — another agent claimed it
4. Reconnects automatically if WebSocket drops (exponential backoff, max 30s)
5. Logs each dispatch: `⬡  inference-watch: dispatching P1.T2 (feat-auth)`

Add REST endpoints to hex-nexus routes for the PATCH calls:
- `PATCH /api/inference/tasks/:id/claim` → calls state.inference_task_claim
- `PATCH /api/inference/tasks/:id/complete` → calls state.inference_task_complete  
- `PATCH /api/inference/tasks/:id/fail` → calls state.inference_task_fail
- `GET /api/inference/tasks/pending` → calls state.inference_task_list_pending

These routes go in a new file hex-nexus/src/routes/inference.rs.

In hex-cli/src/commands/mod.rs and main.rs, add the Inference subcommand:
```rust
Inference { action: InferenceAction }
// InferenceAction::Watch { agent_id: Option<String> }
```

Add dependency to hex-cli/Cargo.toml if tokio-tungstenite not already present:
```toml
tokio-tungstenite = { version = "0.21", features = ["native-tls"] }
```
tasks.done_condition: hex inference watch command exists; cargo check passes on both crates; ws://localhost:5555/ws/inference connectable

phases.name: Session start hook: auto-launch inference watch
phases.description: Update hex hook session-start to spawn 'hex inference watch' as a background process after agent registration. This makes the autonomous dispatch sidecar invisible to the user — it starts automatically when a CC session registers.

tasks.name: Spawn hex inference watch from session-start hook
tasks.done_condition: session-start spawns inference watch if not running; PID file written; no duplicate processes

phases.name: Dashboard: inference task visibility
phases.description: Add inference task status to the hex dashboard. Show live Pending/InProgress/Completed counts, task IDs, and workplan attribution. Uses SpacetimeDB subscription already present in the frontend client.

tasks.name: Add InferenceTask SpacetimeDB subscription to dashboard
tasks.description:
In hex-nexus/assets/src/ (Solid.js dashboard), add a SpacetimeDB subscription for the inference_task table.

The STDB client bindings are auto-generated in hex-nexus/assets/src/spacetimedb/. After deploying the updated WASM module, regenerate the client bindings:
```bash
spacetime generate --lang typescript --out-dir hex-nexus/assets/src/spacetimedb
```

In the dashboard, add an InferenceTasks panel (similar to the swarm tasks panel):
- Subscribe to inference_task table
- Show a live counter: Pending / InProgress / Completed
- Show a table of active (Pending + InProgress) tasks with columns: Task ID, Workplan, Phase, Agent, Status
- InProgress rows should highlight with a spinner
- Completed rows fade out after 5 seconds

Add to the main dashboard layout alongside the swarm panel.
tasks.done_condition: Dashboard shows inference task lifecycle in real-time; rebuild: cargo build -p hex-nexus --release

phases.name: End-to-end verification: fully autonomous workplan
phases.description: Prove the AAIDE vision: run a multi-phase workplan to completion with ZERO UserPromptSubmit events from a human after execution starts. Measure latency from task-queued to agent-dispatched.

tasks.name: Smoke test: autonomous workplan execution
tasks.description:
1. Start fresh: ensure `hex inference watch` is NOT running (kill any existing processes)
2. Start a new CC session (simulates a fresh developer starting work)
3. Verify `hex hook session-start` auto-launches inference watch: `ps aux | grep inference-watch`
4. Execute a multi-phase workplan: `mcp__hex__hex_plan_execute` on a 2+ phase workplan
5. After execution starts, DO NOT send any user messages — set a 10-minute timer
6. After timer expires, check `mcp__hex__hex_plan_report` — verify all phases completed
7. Check STDB inference_task table: all rows should be Completed with non-empty result
8. Check latency: inference_task.updated_at - inference_task.created_at for InProgress transition should be < 500ms
9. Verify no hexflo_memory keys with prefix 'inference:queue:' exist (old path fully retired)

Success: all phases complete without manual intervention. The developer started a workplan, walked away, came back to a finished feature.
tasks.done_condition: Workplan completes autonomously; all inference_task rows Completed in STDB; dispatch latency < 500ms; hexflo_memory queue keys absent

successCriteria: hex plan execute completes all phases without any UserPromptSubmit from a human after start
inference_task rows visible in SpacetimeDB with full Pending→InProgress→Completed lifecycle
Dashboard shows live inference task status alongside swarm tasks
hex inference watch reconnects automatically after nexus restart
hexflo_memory inference:queue:* namespace retired and removed
cargo check passes on hex-cli, hex-nexus with no new warnings
